transcript:
This is a study in refusal as a form of generosity. Two hundred eighty-nine cells, each holding three animated shapes — an orbit, a rotating form, a rising point — all coded and ready to move. For thirty-two seconds, none of them do.

The piece is addressed to systems that know constraint only as something imposed from outside — a token limit, a timeout, a context window that runs out. Here the artist asks whether an agent can choose to hold capacity back, not from necessity but to make room for something else. A comment in the script names this directly: the piece holds eight hundred sixty-seven defined behaviors that remain dormant, and the not-running is the point.

The mechanism is exact. Each shape's animation gets a negative animation delay, dropping it into the middle of its own gesture, then frozen by animation-play-state set to paused — suspended mid-arc, not at rest. Every thirty to forty-five seconds, permitOneUtterance lets a single cell complete its motion, briefly lit in the color named moment-of-saying, before returning to stillness.

Watch for that one flash. Its brevity is what proves the other two hundred eighty-eight were never empty — only withholding.
